description: Cactaceae is a family of flowering plants in the order Caryophyllales, comprising about 175 genera and 1750 species. Almost all cacti are native to the Americas. The family is defined by the areole — a specialised pad from which spines, flowers, and new stems arise. Cacti have evolved extreme drought adaptations including CAM photosynthesis, water-storing stems, and reduced or absent leaves replaced by spines.
meta_description: Shop rare and collectible cacti — Astrophytum, Gymnocalycium, Mammillaria, Ariocarpus. TC-grown with provenance. Species and cultivar selection.
meta_keywords: cacti, Cactaceae, rare cactus, Astrophytum, Mammillaria, Ariocarpus, buy cacti online

genera.description: Astrophytum comprises four species of globular to columnar cacti from Mexico and southern USA. Known as star cacti for their star-shaped cross-section, they are characterised by white trichome flecks on the surface, prominent ribs, and large showy yellow flowers. Among the most highly bred cacti, with hundreds of named cultivars recognised in Japan and internationally.
genera.meta_description: Buy Astrophytum star cacti — myriostigma, asterias, ornatum, and hybrids. TC-grown species and cultivars with provenance from Japanese and Mexican breeding lines.
genera.meta_keywords: Astrophytum for sale, star cactus, Astrophytum myriostigma, Astrophytum asterias, sand dollar cactus
genera.native_range: Mexico (Chihuahuan and Tamaulipan deserts), Southern Texas

species.description: Astrophytum myriostigma is the classic spineless star cactus with 3–7 prominent ribs and a body covered in dense white trichome flecks. Large yellow flowers emerge from the crown. Native to the Chihuahuan Desert. Extensively bred in Japan for body form, rib number, and fleck density.
species.meta_title: Astrophytum myriostigma — Bishop's Cap Star Cactus
species.meta_description: Buy Astrophytum myriostigma, the bishop's cap cactus. Spineless star cactus with white flecks and yellow flowers; Japanese breeding lines and species forms available.
species.meta_keywords: Astrophytum myriostigma, bishop's cap, star cactus, spineless cactus, Japanese cactus
species.notes: Spineless. Excellent drainage essential; tolerates cold if dry. Many Japanese cultivars.

cultivars.description: Four-ribbed form; a natural variant and highly sought collector piece.
Fleckless green body; smooth dark surface without white trichomes — striking minimal form.
Heavily crestated or dimpled surface; a Japanese-selected sculptural form.

species.description: Astrophytum asterias is a flat, disc-shaped spineless cactus from the Rio Grande region of Texas and Tamaulipas, Mexico. It grows flush with the soil surface in the wild, camouflaged among pebbles. CITES Appendix I due to wild collection pressure. Produces bright yellow flowers with red centres. One of the most heavily bred species in Japan.
species.meta_description: Buy Astrophytum asterias, the sand dollar cactus. Flat spineless disc-cactus; CITES I protected. Yellow-red flowers; Japanese Super Kabuto and cultivar forms.
species.meta_keywords: Astrophytum asterias, sand dollar cactus, sea urchin cactus, Super Kabuto, CITES cactus
species.native_range: Rio Grande region — Texas, USA and Tamaulipas, Mexico
species.notes: CITES Appendix I. Flat and disc-shaped. Needs gritty, near-neutral substrate. Spectacular cultivars.

cultivars.description: Dense white trichome clusters forming a regular pattern across the entire surface; premium Japanese selection.
Flower garden form; selected for extra-large and abundantly produced flowers.

genera.description: Gymnocalycium (chin cacti) comprises about 70 species of South American cacti characterised by a 'chin' — a protruding tubercle below each areole. The genus includes many easy-growing species that tolerate lower light than most cacti, making them popular windowsill subjects. Flowers are typically white, pink, or red, and produced reliably even in moderate light.
genera.meta_description: Buy Gymnocalycium chin cacti from South America. Tolerates lower light than most cacti; white, pink or red flowers. Species and colour forms available.
genera.meta_keywords: Gymnocalycium for sale, chin cactus, Gymnocalycium mihanovichii, moon cactus, low-light cactus
genera.native_range: South America (Argentina, Bolivia, Paraguay, Brazil, Uruguay)

species.description: Gymnocalycium mihanovichii is the species from which the famous coloured 'moon cactus' mutants are derived. The chlorophyll-free red, yellow, orange, and pink forms require grafting onto a green stock to survive. The species itself is small, grey-green with banded ribs and pink flowers.
species.meta_title: Gymnocalycium mihanovichii — Chin Cactus and Moon Cactus
species.meta_description: Buy Gymnocalycium mihanovichii species and grafted moon cactus forms. Pink-flowered species and coloured chlorophyll-free mutants grafted on Hylocereus.
species.meta_keywords: Gymnocalycium mihanovichii, moon cactus, chin cactus, grafted cactus, red cactus
species.notes: Species prefers shade; moon cactus mutants are always grafted. Easy and rewarding.

cultivars.description: Chlorophyll-free vivid red mutant; grafted onto Hylocereus stock.
Chlorophyll-free bright yellow mutant; always sold grafted.

species.description: Gymnocalycium baldianum is a compact, freely flowering species from Argentina. Unusual among cacti for its reliably deep red to carmine flowers produced in summer. Small flat body; tolerate partial shade. One of the most popular Gymnocalycium in cultivation.
species.meta_description: Buy Gymnocalycium baldianum, the red-flowered chin cactus from Argentina. Deep red to carmine summer flowers on a compact flat body; easy grower.
species.meta_keywords: Gymnocalycium baldianum, red flower cactus, dwarf chin cactus, Argentina cactus
species.notes: One of few cacti with deep red flowers. Tolerates partial shade. Compact and easy.

species.description: Gymnocalycium saglionis is the largest species in the genus, developing a wide, flattened globe up to 30 cm in diameter. Strong curved spines and white to pale pink flowers. A long-lived specimen plant from northwestern Argentina.
species.meta_description: Buy Gymnocalycium saglionis, the giant chin cactus. Large flattened globe to 30 cm; strong curved spines and pale pink flowers from NW Argentina.
species.meta_keywords: Gymnocalycium saglionis, giant chin cactus, large Gymnocalycium, specimen cactus
species.notes: Grows large over time. Excellent specimen plant; long-lived and robust.

genera.description: Mammillaria is the largest genus in Cactaceae with over 200 species, all from Mexico and the surrounding region. Characterised by spirally arranged tubercles (not ribs) bearing areoles at the tips. Small flowers are produced in a ring or crown around the top of the plant, often followed by attractive red club-shaped fruit. The most popular cactus genus for collectors.
genera.meta_description: Buy Mammillaria pincushion cacti — 200+ species from Mexico. Crown of small flowers, hooked spines, red fruit. Easy windowsill cacti for beginners and collectors.
genera.meta_keywords: Mammillaria for sale, pincushion cactus, Mammillaria hahniana, Mammillaria elongata, Mexican cactus
genera.native_range: Mexico, southwest USA, Caribbean, Central America

species.description: Mammillaria hahniana is densely covered in long white hair-like spines giving it a woolly, soft appearance. Produces a ring of small carmine-pink flowers around the crown in spring, followed by tiny red fruit. One of the most beloved and easy Mammillaria species for beginners.
species.meta_description: Buy Mammillaria hahniana, the old lady cactus. Dense white woolly spines with a ring of pink spring flowers; classic easy beginner cactus.
species.meta_keywords: Mammillaria hahniana, old lady cactus, woolly cactus, pink-flowered cactus, beginner cactus
species.notes: Excellent beginner cactus. Dense white spines; pink flower crown in spring.

species.description: Mammillaria elongata is a clustering species forming mounds of cylindrical stems covered in golden to reddish radial spines arranged in star patterns. Small cream to pale yellow flowers. Highly variable spine colour across its range. Very easy and fast-growing.
species.meta_description: Buy Mammillaria elongata, the gold lace cactus. Clustering cylindrical stems with star-pattern golden spines; fast-growing and easy for beginners.
species.meta_keywords: Mammillaria elongata, gold lace cactus, clustering cactus, easy cactus, Mexican Mammillaria
species.notes: Very easy and fast-growing. Clusters freely; variable spine colour from gold to red-brown.

species.description: Mammillaria plumosa produces clusters of soft, feathery white plume-like spines that are completely soft to the touch — unlike virtually every other cactus. Produces small white to pale pink flowers with a delicate scent. Native to Nuevo León, Mexico.
species.meta_description: Buy Mammillaria plumosa, the feather cactus. Soft plume-like white spines; completely spineless to touch. Fragrant white flowers. Unusual and safe cacti.
species.meta_keywords: Mammillaria plumosa, feather cactus, soft spine cactus, child-safe cactus, fluffy cactus
species.notes: Unique among cacti — spines are soft and feathery. Safe for children. Fragrant flowers.

genera.description: Ariocarpus comprises six species of extremely slow-growing Mexican cacti known as living rocks. The plants are deeply embedded in the soil, showing only a flat rosette of fleshy, wrinkled or tuberculate areoles that mimic the surrounding rocks. CITES Appendix I. Among the most sought-after and difficult collector cacti, requiring perfect drainage, full sun, and patience.
genera.meta_description: Buy Ariocarpus living rock cacti from Mexico. Extremely slow-growing CITES I species that mimic surrounding rocks. TC-grown with full provenance.
genera.meta_keywords: Ariocarpus for sale, living rock cactus, Ariocarpus retusus, CITES cactus, rare Mexican cactus

species.description: Ariocarpus retusus is the most widespread living rock cactus, found across a wide range of limestone habitats in northern Mexico. The flat rosette of truncate, greyish tubercles blends perfectly with surrounding rocks and soil. White to pale pink flowers emerge from the woolly centre in autumn. CITES Appendix I.
species.meta_title: Ariocarpus retusus — Seven Stars Living Rock Cactus
species.meta_description: Buy Ariocarpus retusus, the seven stars living rock. Flat grey tubercle rosette mimics surrounding rock; autumn-flowering CITES I collector species.
species.meta_keywords: Ariocarpus retusus, seven stars living rock, CITES cactus, flat cactus, rock-mimicking cactus
species.native_range: Northern Mexico (Coahuila, Nuevo León, Tamaulipas, San Luis Potosí)
species.notes: CITES I. Extremely slow; years to reach flowering size. Perfect drainage and full sun essential.

species.description: Ariocarpus fissuratus is a critically important living rock cactus with flat, deeply fissured, star-shaped tubercles. The deeply cracked grey-brown surface creates exceptional camouflage among desert gravel. Pink to magenta flowers. Known as peyote cimarrón due to mescaline content. CITES Appendix I.
species.meta_description: Buy Ariocarpus fissuratus, the chautle living rock. Deeply fissured star-shaped tubercles; extraordinary rock camouflage. CITES I, TC-grown stock only.
species.meta_keywords: Ariocarpus fissuratus, chautle living rock, fissured cactus, CITES I cactus, rock cactus Texas
species.notes: CITES I. Among slowest-growing cacti. Contains mescaline. Requires perfect drainage and intense light.
